annotations.label: Lattice in real coordinate space

anchor.snippet: is an infinite set of points in this space with these properties

annotations.note: IsZLattice K L characterizes a discrete Z-submodule whose K-span is the ambient space, generalizing the R^n definition.

annotations.label: Square lattice and integer lattices

anchor.snippet: One of the simplest examples of a lattice is the square lattice

annotations.note: The Z-span of the standard basis in ι → ℝ is an IsZLattice instance, giving the integer lattice; no named `squareLattice` decl exists.

annotations.label: Lattice is a Delone set

anchor.snippet: The requirements of minimum and maximum distance can be summarized by saying that a lattice is a Delone set

annotations.note: Delone sets are defined but there is no lemma exhibiting a ℤ-lattice as a Delone set.

annotations.label: Lattice as free abelian group spanning vector space

anchor.snippet: a lattice can be described as a free abelian group of dimension

annotations.note: ZLattice.module_free states that any IsZLattice is a free ℤ-module, complementing IsZLattice.span_top for the spanning condition.

annotations.label: Lattice as symmetry group of discrete translational symmetry

anchor.snippet: A lattice is the symmetry group of discrete translational symmetry in n directions

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a translation-symmetry group of a subset of Euclidean space, so this characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lattice is a finitely generated free abelian group

anchor.snippet: As a group (dropping its geometric structure) a lattice is a finitely generated free abelian group

annotations.note: ZLattice.module_finite gives Module.Finite ℤ L and ZLattice.module_free gives freeness, jointly witnessing the finitely generated free abelian group structure.

annotations.label: E8 and Leech lattices

anchor.snippet: More complicated examples include the E 8 lattice

annotations.note: Grep for E8/Leech turns up only ADE-inequality/root-system material, with no lattice construction.

annotations.label: Change of basis by integer transition matrix

anchor.snippet: Any other basis

annotations.note: The general fact that a change-of-basis determinant is a unit specializes over ℤ to ±1 (unimodular), but no ZLattice-specific statement is stated.

annotations.label: Fundamental domain / primitive cell

anchor.snippet: copies of the n -dimensional parallelepiped spanned by the basis vectors, known as the fundamental domain or primitive cell of the lattice

annotations.note: ZSpan.fundamentalDomain builds the parallelepiped for a basis and ZSpan.isAddFundamentalDomain shows it is a fundamental domain of the lattice action.

annotations.label: Covolume and unimodular lattice

anchor.snippet: The n -dimensional volume of this fundamental domain is sometimes called the covolume of the lattice

annotations.note: ZLattice.covolume defines the covolume; there is no `Unimodular` predicate for lattices in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minkowski's theorem

anchor.snippet: Minkowski's theorem relates the number

annotations.note: This file states the Minkowski Convex Body Theorem in both strict and compact-domain forms.

annotations.label: Ehrhart polynomial of a lattice polytope

anchor.snippet: the number of lattice points it contains is described by the polytope's Ehrhart polynomial

annotations.note: Grep for `Ehrhart` finds no Mathlib decls.

annotations.label: LLL lattice basis reduction algorithm

anchor.snippet: the Lenstra–Lenstra–Lovász lattice basis reduction algorithm

annotations.note: No LLL/Lenstra basis-reduction material exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Crystallographic restriction (five 2D lattice types)

anchor.section: Lattices in two dimensions: detailed discussion
anchor.snippet: There are five 2D lattice types as given by the crystallographic restriction theorem

annotations.note: IsCrystallographic exists only for root pairings; the 2D classification into five Bravais types is not present.

annotations.label: Period lattice and basis in 2D

anchor.section: Lattices in two dimensions: detailed discussion
anchor.snippet: The general case is known as a period lattice

annotations.note: PeriodPair.lattice defines the ℤ-submodule of ℂ generated by two periods ω₁, ω₂.

annotations.label: Fundamental parallelogram in 2D

anchor.section: Lattices in two dimensions: detailed discussion
anchor.snippet: Each basis pair { p , q } defines a parallelogram, all with the same area given by the magnitude of the cross product

annotations.note: The general fundamental parallelepiped and its basis-independent volume via ZLattice.covolume_eq_det specialize to the 2D area-by-cross-product fact, but the 2D statement is not called out separately.

annotations.label: Complex representation and modular group action

anchor.section: Lattices in two dimensions: detailed discussion
anchor.snippet: The basis vectors { p,q } can be represented by complex numbers

annotations.note: SL(2,ℤ) and its action on the upper half-plane are formalized, but no statement identifies its orbits with equivalence classes of 2D lattices.

annotations.label: 14 Bravais lattices in 3D

anchor.snippet: The 14 lattice types in 3D are called Bravais lattices

annotations.note: Bravais lattices are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lattice in complex space

anchor.snippet: is a discrete subgroup of

annotations.note: IsZLattice K L applies with K = ℝ and E = ℂ^n, capturing a discrete Z-submodule whose ℝ-span (dim 2n) is the whole space.

annotations.label: Rank of a lattice in complex n-space

anchor.snippet: will be a free abelian group of rank

annotations.note: ZLattice.rank K L states finrank ℤ L = finrank K E, which specializes to 2n over ℝ for E = ℂ^n.

annotations.label: Gaussian integers as a lattice

anchor.snippet: the Gaussian integers

annotations.note: GaussianInt = ℤ[i] is defined as a ring, but there is no explicit IsZLattice instance exhibiting its image in ℂ as a lattice.

annotations.label: Lattice in a Lie group

anchor.snippet: a lattice Γ in a Lie group G is a discrete subgroup

annotations.note: No general Lie-group lattice notion (discrete subgroup with finite Haar-covolume quotient) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform / cocompact lattice

anchor.snippet: A lattice is said to be uniform or cocompact

annotations.note: The concept of a cocompact/uniform lattice in a Lie group is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: R-lattice in a vector space

anchor.snippet: Then the R lattice

annotations.note: Submodule.IsLattice A M asserts that an R-submodule M of a K-vector space is finitely generated with K-span equal to the whole space.

annotations.label: Lattices generated by GL-related bases are isomorphic

anchor.snippet: then the lattices generated by these bases will be isomorphic

annotations.note: General basis-change-is-unit results specialize to give the equivalence of lattices related by an invertible R-matrix, but no dedicated lemma is stated for R-lattices.

annotations.label: Dual lattice in inner product space

anchor.snippet: For a vector space which is also an inner product space , the dual lattice can be concretely described by the set

annotations.note: The dual submodule with respect to a bilinear form is defined generally; applied to the inner-product form on a lattice this gives the dual lattice.

annotations.label: Primitive element of a lattice

anchor.snippet: A primitive element of a lattice is an element

annotations.note: No `primitive element of a lattice` notion (indivisible vector in a ℤ-lattice) is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Primitive generator of one-dimensional sublattice

anchor.snippet: Every one-dimensional sublattice

annotations.note: The statement that every rank-one sublattice is generated by a primitive vector is not present.

annotations.label: Saturated sublattice (equivalent conditions)

anchor.snippet: is a saturated sublattice whenever any of the following equivalent conditions holds

annotations.note: The abstract saturated-subgroup notion (n·g ∈ H ⇒ n=0 ∨ g ∈ H) generalizes saturation for sublattices, but the multiple equivalent conditions bundle is not spelled out.
